pr.title: Clean up item article data types and accesses
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Replaced several ad-hoc item article wrapper structs and local extern declarations with typed Article** globals declared in it_266F.h, then simplified common, character, Pokemon, and stage item article table accesses to direct array indexing. The cleanup also typed StageInfo.x6B4 as an array of script pointers and introduced ItLGunVars for the light gun special attributes access. This mattered because it removed misleading placeholder structs and made item article table indexing clearer and more reusable across item and ground code.
what_changed: Deleted placeholder article container types from src/melee/it/types.h, including CommonItemArticles, UnkItemArticles, UnkItemArticles2, UnkItemArticles3, and their nested unknown structs.
Changed it_804D6D24 from CommonItemArticles* to Article** and added header declarations for it_804D6D30, it_804D6D38, and it_804A0F60 as Article** or Article* array globals in src/melee/it/it_266F.h.

Updated Item_80267978 to retrieve article data with direct indexing: it_804D6D24[item_data->kind], it_804D6D38[idx], it_804D6D30[idx], and it_804A0F60[idx], instead of going through wrapper fields such as article_ptr or unkptr.

Updated it_8026B3F8 to store character item article pointers with it_804D6D38[kind - It_Kind_Kuriboh] = article, replacing an r13_ItemTable-style access that was explicitly commented as feeling wrong.
Updated it_8026B40C to store stage item article pointers directly into it_804A0F60[kind - It_Kind_Old_Kuri].

Changed it_8026C1D4 from an unknown nested common item article access to a typed light gun special-attributes access: ItLGunVars* lgun = it_804D6D24[It_Kind_L_Gun]->x4_specialAttributes; return lgun->x0.
Added ItLGunVars in src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h as a small struct containing u32 x0.
Changed StageInfo.x6B4 in src/melee/gr/types.h from u32* to unk_t** and updated Ground_801C0800 to compare entries against NULL and assign stage_info.x6B4[i] into Article item state script fields.
Removed file-local extern declarations for item article globals from ground.c, item.c, and it_26B1.c in favor of shared header declarations.
smart_moves: Recognized that the article tables were better represented as arrays of Article* rather than bespoke wrapper structs with unkptr fields, which made the code match its actual usage.
Centralized global article table declarations in it_266F.h, reducing duplicated and inconsistent extern declarations across source files.

Used item kind base constants as index origins, preserving the existing logical partitioning of item ranges: common items, character items starting at It_Kind_Kuriboh, Pokemon starting at Pokemon_Tosakinto, and stage items starting at It_Kind_Old_Kuri.
Converted the light gun access from an opaque nested x40/x4/x0 path to an Article special-attributes cast, documenting the real data relationship with ItLGunVars.
Improved pointer semantics in stage data by changing StageInfo.x6B4 to unk_t** and using NULL comparisons rather than integer zero comparisons.
decomp_lessons: When a placeholder struct only exists to make array indexing compile, check whether the underlying global is actually an array of pointers; a direct Article** type can often remove multiple fake wrapper types.
Avoid scattering local extern declarations for globals. Put them in the relevant header once so all translation units agree on the type.
Comments such as 'This feels very wrong' are useful indicators that a struct model may be fighting the actual data layout; revisit them when adjacent symbols become better understood.
For item article data, preserve the enum-range offset math in the code because it documents which article table is being indexed.
If an Article field is known to point to item-specific special attributes, introducing a small per-item vars struct can make accesses clearer without requiring a fully complete type.
Pointer-valued stage arrays should be typed and compared as pointers, not as u32 sentinel arrays, when the code is passing entries into script pointer fields.
naming_conventions: Article table globals use the existing DOL symbol names but are annotated by table role in comments: it_804D6D24 for common items, it_804D6D30 for Pokemon items, it_804D6D38 for character items, and it_804A0F60 for stage items.
Per-item special-attribute structs follow the item-name-plus-Vars style, as shown by the new ItLGunVars alongside existing FoodVars and EggVars.
Unknown stage script pointer data remains conservatively named x6B4, but its type was improved to unk_t** rather than being renamed without stronger evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Direct Article** indexing can replace equivalent wrapper-field dereferences while preserving the same address arithmetic, making code simpler without changing behavior.
Use enum subtraction constants to express table offsets in a way that should still match generated indexing code.
Casting Article->x4_specialAttributes to a small known vars struct is a practical tactic for matching accesses to item-specific data before the full special-attributes layout is known.
Changing integer pointer arrays to pointer-typed arrays can improve C expression shape, such as NULL sentinel checks and assignment into pointer fields, without inventing new symbol names.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further rename or type Article, x4_specialAttributes, or item-specific Vars structs.
Search for remaining local extern declarations of it_804D6D24, it_804D6D30, it_804D6D38, or it_804A0F60.
Check whether StageInfo.x6B4 is later given a more specific script pointer type.
Look for remaining fake wrapper structs whose only purpose is exposing unkptr or article_ptr arrays.
